sentences.quote: We report a Lean~4 formalization that identifies the Higgs bilinear of the electroweak Standard Model with the scalar channel of the Wetterich-NJL four-fermion interaction already verified in our formalization base (\texttt{WetterichNJL.lean}, \texttt{TetradGapEquation.lean}, \texttt{ADWMechanism.lean}, \texttt{FermiPointTopology.lean}).
sentences.quote_normalized: we report a lean~4 formalization that identifies the higgs bilinear of the electroweak standard model with the scalar channel of the wetterich-njl four-fermion interaction already verified in our formalization base (wetterichnjl.lean, tetradgapequation.lean, adwmechanism.lean, fermipointtopology.lean).
sentences.agent_notes: Metaclaim about the paper's contribution. WetterichNJL.lean, TetradGapEquation.lean, ADWMechanism.lean, FermiPointTopology.lean all exist as Lean modules.

sentences.quote: The identification is structural in the flavor-singlet frame---an electroweak $SU(2)_L\times U(1)_Y$ index dressing remains deep-research-gated---and yields three formal consequences:
sentences.quote_normalized: the identification is structural in the flavor-singlet frame---an electroweak $su(2)_l\times u(1)_y$ index dressing remains deep-research-gated---and yields three formal consequences:

sentences.quote: (i)~the Mexican-hat potential $V(\HiggsField) = -\tfrac{1}{2}\mu^2|\HiggsField|^2 + \tfrac{1}{4}\lambda|\HiggsField|^4$ arises as a specialization of the supercritical branch of the tetrad gap equation;
sentences.quote_normalized: (i)~the mexican-hat potential $v(\higgsfield) = -\tfrac{1}{2}\mu^2|\higgsfield|^2 + \tfrac{1}{4}\lambda|\higgsfield|^4$ arises as a specialization of the supercritical branch of the tetrad gap equation;
sentences.agent_notes: Mexican-hat-from-tetrad bridge encoded as tracked external hypothesis H_ScalarChannelIsTetradBifurcationOutput; consequence theorems mexican_hat_vev_under_supercritical_bridge and bridge_excludes_super_uv_vev exist in lean_deps.json.

sentences.quote: (ii)~the Anderson-Higgs $W$/$Z$ mass matrix $M_W = gv/2$, $M_Z = \sqrt{g^2+g'^2}\,v/2$ follows directly from the scalar-channel VEV $v = \sqrt{\mu^2/\lambda}$ with $\cos\theta_W = M_W/M_Z$;
sentences.quote_normalized: (ii)~the anderson-higgs $w$/$z$ mass matrix $m_w = gv/2$, $m_z = \sqrt{g^2+g'^2}\,v/2$ follows directly from the scalar-channel vev $v = \sqrt{\mu^2/\lambda}$ with $\cos\theta_w = m_w/m_z$;
sentences.agent_notes: Mass-matrix theorem and cos(theta_W) identity formalized as ew_mass_matrix_from_scalar_vev / wMass_div_zMass; both exist.

sentences.quote: and (iii)~Yukawa couplings reduce to overlap integrals on the FermiPointTopology emergent Weyl modes, with linearity and zero-overlap-zero-coupling lemmas formalized as a stand-in for the microscopic overlap-integral form (deep-research-gated, see Open Question O.2).
sentences.quote_normalized: and (iii)~yukawa couplings reduce to overlap integrals on the fermipointtopology emergent weyl modes, with linearity and zero-overlap-zero-coupling lemmas formalized as a stand-in for the microscopic overlap-integral form (deep-research-gated, see open question o.2).
sentences.agent_notes: Linearity and zero-coupling-iff-zero-overlap lemmas formalized at stand-in scope; microscopic form gated on Open Question O.2 (disclosed). Both theorems exist.

sentences.quote: The correctness-push anchor is a microscopic Higgs-mass prediction $m_H = \sqrt{2\mu^2}$ from the Wetterich scalar-channel gap equation: under the schematic leading-log substrate closure, this prediction reaches $m_H = 125.25$~GeV only along a one-parameter curve in the $(\Lambda_{\rm UV},\,G_c)$ plane, requiring tuning rather than emerging from a generic substrate---a structural-only verdict at the current formalization scope.
sentences.quote_normalized: the correctness-push anchor is a microscopic higgs-mass prediction $m_h = \sqrt{2\mu^2}$ from the wetterich scalar-channel gap equation: under the schematic leading-log substrate closure, this prediction reaches $m_h = 125.25$~gev only along a one-parameter curve in the $(\lambda_{\rm uv},\,g_c)$ plane, requiring tuning rather than emerging from a generic substrate---a structural-only verdict at the current formalization scope.
sentences.agent_notes: Qualitative summary of figure heatmap result; positivity theorem exists. Visual claim about '1D curve' delegated to figure-reviewer.

sentences.quote: The complete formalization lives in a single new Lean module (\texttt{ScalarRungInterpretation.lean}), consisting of $20$~theorems with zero sorry, zero new axioms, and a Python cross-validation layer covering every theorem (the Lean module replaces three vacuous Props with substantive ones during a project-required anti-tautology audit; the audit also adds a tracked-hypothesis bridge for the TetradGapEquation identification, in line with project precedent).
sentences.quote_normalized: the complete formalization lives in a single new lean module (scalarrunginterpretation.lean), consisting of $20$~theorems with zero sorry, zero new axioms, and a python cross-validation layer covering every theorem (the lean module replaces three vacuous props with substantive ones during a project-required anti-tautology audit; the audit also adds a tracked-hypothesis bridge for the tetradgapequation identification, in line with project precedent).

sentences.quote: We formulate the falsifiability anchor as a decidable Lean predicate and report that, in the flavor-singlet frame, the scalar-rung framing is a \emph{structural} identification of EWSB rather than a quantitative prediction of the observed $125.25$~GeV Higgs mass.
sentences.quote_normalized: we formulate the falsifiability anchor as a decidable lean predicate and report that, in the flavor-singlet frame, the scalar-rung framing is a structural identification of ewsb rather than a quantitative prediction of the observed $125.25$~gev higgs mass.

sentences.quote: The fluid-based emergent-gravity program developed in this research project formalizes a Landau hierarchy of fermion-bilinear condensates on a single fermionic substrate~\cite{ADW,WetterichSpinor2013,WetterichSpinor2022,Volovik2024}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the fluid-based emergent-gravity program developed in this research project formalizes a landau hierarchy of fermion-bilinear condensates on a single fermionic substrate~.
sentences.agent_notes: All four citations present in CITATION_REGISTRY.

sentences.quote: Two rungs of this hierarchy have been formalized in prior phases: the disordered $\to$ vestigial-metric transition (\texttt{VestigialSusceptibility.lean}, $16$ theorems) and the vestigial-metric $\to$ tetrad transition (\texttt{TetradGapEquation.lean}, $20$ theorems; \texttt{ADWMechanism.lean}, $21$ theorems).
sentences.quote_normalized: two rungs of this hierarchy have been formalized in prior phases: the disordered $\to$ vestigial-metric transition (vestigialsusceptibility.lean, $16$ theorems) and the vestigial-metric $\to$ tetrad transition (tetradgapequation.lean, $20$ theorems; adwmechanism.lean, $21$ theorems).

sentences.quote: Both rungs share the Wetterich-NJL four-fermion infrastructure (\texttt{WetterichNJL.lean}, $18$ theorems) and the FermiPointTopology emergent-Weyl substrate (\texttt{FermiPointTopology.lean}, $33$ theorems).
sentences.quote_normalized: both rungs share the wetterich-njl four-fermion infrastructure (wetterichnjl.lean, $18$ theorems) and the fermipointtopology emergent-weyl substrate (fermipointtopology.lean, $33$ theorems).

sentences.quote: In this paper we add a third rung: the \emph{scalar rung}, identified with the Higgs bilinear of the electroweak Standard Model.
sentences.quote_normalized: in this paper we add a third rung: the scalar rung, identified with the higgs bilinear of the electroweak standard model.
sentences.agent_notes: Metaclaim; supported by scalar_rung_is_landau_rung theorem.

sentences.quote: The identification is the natural completion of the Wetterich-NJL Fierz decomposition---the scalar channel ($\Gamma = \mathbb{1}$ in the spacetime Clifford basis) is the only flavor-singlet attractive channel that drives a parity-even condensate.
sentences.quote_normalized: the identification is the natural completion of the wetterich-njl fierz decomposition---the scalar channel ($\gamma = \mathbb{1}$ in the spacetime clifford basis) is the only flavor-singlet attractive channel that drives a parity-even condensate.
sentences.agent_notes: Qualitative attribution; Fierz decomposition formalized via fierz_completeness. Uniqueness assertion is physics interpretation.

sentences.quote: If the same scalar channel is identified with $\HiggsField^\dagger \HiggsField$ on an $SU(2)_L$-doublet-extended substrate, the Mexican-hat potential, the Anderson-Higgs $W$/$Z$ mass matrix, and Yukawa couplings as overlap integrals all emerge as direct consequences of the existing formalization infrastructure---without new physics input.
sentences.quote_normalized: if the same scalar channel is identified with $\higgsfield^\dagger \higgsfield$ on an $su(2)_l$-doublet-extended substrate, the mexican-hat potential, the anderson-higgs $w$/$z$ mass matrix, and yukawa couplings as overlap integrals all emerge as direct consequences of the existing formalization infrastructure---without new physics input.
sentences.agent_notes: Conditional ('if ... then ...') framing; full SU(2)_L extension gated on O.2.

sentences.quote: The identification is at structural scope because the $SU(2)_L\times U(1)_Y$ electroweak index structure of the Wetterich-NJL scalar channel is currently deep-research gated.
sentences.quote_normalized: the identification is at structural scope because the $su(2)_l\times u(1)_y$ electroweak index structure of the wetterich-njl scalar channel is currently deep-research gated.
sentences.agent_notes: Scope disclosure for deep-research gate.

sentences.quote: We therefore work in the \emph{flavor-singlet frame}: the scalar channel carries no electroweak indices; the Higgs identification is structural in the sense that it commutes with the gauge-singlet projection of the SM Higgs.
sentences.quote_normalized: we therefore work in the flavor-singlet frame: the scalar channel carries no electroweak indices; the higgs identification is structural in the sense that it commutes with the gauge-singlet projection of the sm higgs.

sentences.quote: The $SU(2)_L\times U(1)_Y$-dressed extension is documented as a Wave~1 follow-up gated on Open Question O.2 in the Phase 5z roadmap (\texttt{Lit-Search/Tasks/Phase5z\_wetterich\_njl\_ew\_index\_structure.md}).

sentences.quote: \textbf{Contributions.} (i)~The first formal-verification-grade identification of the Higgs bilinear with a Wetterich-NJL scalar channel, formalized in Lean~4 in the flavor-singlet frame (\texttt{ScalarRungInterpretation.lean}, $20$ theorems, zero sorry).
sentences.quote_normalized: contributions. (i)~the first formal-verification-grade identification of the higgs bilinear with a wetterich-njl scalar channel, formalized in lean~4 in the flavor-singlet frame (scalarrunginterpretation.lean, $20$ theorems, zero sorry).

sentences.quote: The Wetterich-NJL four-fermion interaction~\cite{WetterichNJL} on a fermionic substrate $\psi_x$ admits the Fierz decomposition with $\Gamma_\alpha\in\{\mathbb{1},\gamma_5,\gamma_\mu,\gamma_\mu\gamma_5,\sigma_{\mu\nu}\}$ and the Fierz-completeness identity $1 + 1 + 4 + 4 + 6 = 16 = 4^2$~\cite{Fierz}, formalized as \texttt{WetterichNJL.fierz\_completeness} in our base.
sentences.quote_normalized: the wetterich-njl four-fermion interaction~ on a fermionic substrate $\psi_x$ admits the fierz decomposition with $\gamma_\alpha\in\{\mathbb{1},\gamma_5,\gamma_\mu,\gamma_\mu\gamma_5,\sigma_{\mu\nu}\}$ and the fierz-completeness identity $1 + 1 + 4 + 4 + 6 = 16 = 4^2$~, formalized as wetterichnjl.fierz\_completeness in our base.

sentences.quote: The scalar channel ($\Gamma_\alpha = \mathbb{1}$) is the unique flavor-singlet attractive channel in the Fierz basis and drives the leading parity-even condensation~\cite{NJL61}.
sentences.quote_normalized: the scalar channel ($\gamma_\alpha = \mathbb{1}$) is the unique flavor-singlet attractive channel in the fierz basis and drives the leading parity-even condensation~.

sentences.quote: The Python implementation \texttt{higgs\_mass\_from\_vev} matches PDG~2024 within RG-running uncertainty: $\mu^2 = \lambda v^2$ with $\lambda = 0.129$ and $v = 246.22$~GeV gives $m_H = 125.06$~GeV, against the observed $125.25 \pm 0.17$~GeV.
sentences.quote_normalized: the python implementation higgs\_mass\_from\_vev matches pdg~2024 within rg-running uncertainty: $\mu^2 = \lambda v^2$ with $\lambda = 0.129$ and $v = 246.22$~gev gives $m_h = 125.06$~gev, against the observed $125.25 \pm 0.17$~gev.
sentences.agent_notes: Recompute m_H = sqrt(2*0.129)*246.22 = 125.0642 GeV; paper says 125.06. delta_pct=0.003%. Within 0.5% tolerance.

sentences.quote: The scalar-channel VEV provides the single dimensionful input to the Anderson-Higgs $W$/$Z$ mass matrix. The Lean theorem \texttt{ScalarRungInterpretation.ew\_mass\_matrix\_from\_scalar\_vev} states the bundled result: $M_W = (1/2) g v$, $M_Z = (1/2) \sqrt{g^2 + g'^2} v$, with $g$ the $SU(2)_L$ gauge coupling, $g'$ the $U(1)_Y$ hypercharge coupling, and $v$ the scalar-channel VEV.
sentences.quote_normalized: the scalar-channel vev provides the single dimensionful input to the anderson-higgs $w$/$z$ mass matrix. the lean theorem scalarrunginterpretation.ew\_mass\_matrix\_from\_scalar\_vev states the bundled result: $m_w = (1/2) g v$, $m_z = (1/2) \sqrt{g^2 + g'^2} v$, with $g$ the $su(2)_l$ gauge coupling, $g'$ the $u(1)_y$ hypercharge coupling, and $v$ the scalar-channel vev.
sentences.agent_notes: ew_mass_matrix_from_scalar_vev exists; bundled M_W and M_Z formulas. Match.

sentences.quote: The Lean signature is parameterized over the \texttt{EWMassMatrixInputs} structure; the positivity theorems \texttt{wMass\_pos} and \texttt{zMass\_pos} ensure both masses are strictly positive whenever $g > 0$ and $v > 0$.
sentences.quote_normalized: the lean signature is parameterized over the ewmassmatrixinputs structure; the positivity theorems wmass\_pos and zmass\_pos ensure both masses are strictly positive whenever $g > 0$ and $v > 0$.

sentences.quote: The on-shell weak-mixing identity $M_W/M_Z = g/\sqrt{g^2+g'^2} = \cos\theta_W$ is formalized as \texttt{wMass\_div\_zMass} (when $g'>0$).
sentences.quote_normalized: the on-shell weak-mixing identity $m_w/m_z = g/\sqrt{g^2+g'^2} = \cos\theta_w$ is formalized as wmass\_div\_zmass (when $g'>0$).
sentences.agent_notes: wMass_div_zMass exists.

sentences.quote: Two strengthened forms address the bound: \texttt{zMass\_ge\_wMass} establishes the weak inequality $M_Z \geq M_W$ for any $g'\geq 0$ (no positivity strictness required), and \texttt{wMass\_lt\_zMass\_of\_g'\_pos} gives the strict inequality when $g' > 0$.
sentences.quote_normalized: two strengthened forms address the bound: zmass\_ge\_wmass establishes the weak inequality $m_z \geq m_w$ for any $g'\geq 0$ (no positivity strictness required), and wmass\_lt\_zmass\_of\_g'\_pos gives the strict inequality when $g' > 0$.
sentences.agent_notes: Both exist.

sentences.quote: Together these formalize the fact that the on-shell weak mixing angle is in $(0,\pi/2)$ exactly when both gauge couplings are nonzero.
sentences.quote_normalized: together these formalize the fact that the on-shell weak mixing angle is in $(0,\pi/2)$ exactly when both gauge couplings are nonzero.
sentences.agent_notes: Interpretive consequence statement.

sentences.quote: \textbf{Numerical consistency.} Using the PDG~2024 values $g = 0.6536$, $g' = 0.3489$, $v = 246.22$~GeV, the Python wrapper \texttt{anderson\_higgs\_w\_z\_masses} produces $M_W = 80.47$~GeV (PDG: $80.37$~GeV) and $M_Z = 91.21$~GeV (PDG: $91.19$~GeV)---both within $0.2\%$, well inside the running-coupling slop.
sentences.quote_normalized: numerical consistency. using the pdg~2024 values $g = 0.6536$, $g' = 0.3489$, $v = 246.22$~gev, the python wrapper anderson\_higgs\_w\_z\_masses produces $m_w = 80.47$~gev (pdg: $80.37$~gev) and $m_z = 91.21$~gev (pdg: $91.19$~gev)---both within $0.2\%$, well inside the running-coupling slop.
sentences.agent_notes: Recompute: M_W = 0.5*0.6536*246.22 = 80.4647 GeV (paper: 80.47). M_Z = 0.5*sqrt(0.6536^2+0.3489^2)*246.22 = 91.2115 GeV (paper: 91.21). PDG-vs-recomputed deltas: M_W 0.12%, M_Z 0.02% — both <0.2% as paper asserts.

sentences.quote: The $\cos\theta_W = 0.882$ matches both the on-shell $M_W/M_Z$ ratio and the $\sqrt{1 - \sin^2\theta_W}$ value from the PDG effective-scheme $\sin^2\theta_W = 0.231$ within $1\%$.

sentences.quote: The third structural consequence of the scalar-rung identification is that Yukawa couplings reduce to overlap integrals on the FermiPointTopology emergent Weyl modes.

sentences.quote: Formally, the Lean \texttt{WeylOverlap} structure carries a real-valued overlap density $\rho_{fg}$ and a substrate normalization $N$, and the Yukawa coupling is the linear functional $y_f = \rho_{fg} N$.

sentences.quote: The Phase 5z Gate~Z.1 correctness-push anchor is a microscopic prediction for the Higgs mass from the Wetterich scalar-channel gap equation. Under the schematic leading-log substrate closure $m_H = \sqrt{2\lambda_4} \Lambda_{UV} \exp(-\pi^2/(2 N_f G_c))$, where $\Lambda_{UV}$ is the UV cutoff, $N_f$ is the Weyl-component count, $G_c$ is the dimensionless 4-fermion coupling, and $\lambda_4$ is the scalar-channel quartic, the Lean theorem \texttt{higgsMassFromCondensate\_pos} establishes $m_H > 0$ for positive substrate parameters.
sentences.quote_normalized: the phase 5z gate~z.1 correctness-push anchor is a microscopic prediction for the higgs mass from the wetterich scalar-channel gap equation. under the schematic leading-log substrate closure $m_h = \sqrt{2\lambda_4} \lambda_{uv} \exp(-\pi^2/(2 n_f g_c))$, where $\lambda_{uv}$ is the uv cutoff, $n_f$ is the weyl-component count, $g_c$ is the dimensionless 4-fermion coupling, and $\lambda_4$ is the scalar-channel quartic, the lean theorem higgsmassfromcondensate\_pos establishes $m_h > 0$ for positive substrate parameters.
